news_templates.template: TRADE ALERT: {faction} imposes trade embargo — {description}
COMMERCE: Embargo restricts trade at {system} — smuggling premiums spike
EDITORIAL: {faction} embargo enters second week. Independent haulers bear the cost. As usual.
COMMERCE: Stations near {system} report empty shelves on essential goods. {faction} calls shortages 'temporary adjustments.'
SECURITY: Three cargo vessels seized near {system} under embargo enforcement. Crews detained pending review.
MARKETS: Black market prices near {system} triple overnight. Official channels remain 'fully operational,' per {faction} spokesperson.
CULTURE: {description}
MARKETS: Festival at {system} drives luxury demand — traders flock to the exchange
CULTURE: Celebrations at {system} draw thousands. Local merchants extend hours. Pickpocket reports also up.
COMMERCE: Festival at {system} boosts local economy. Workers granted half-shift holiday — unpaid, naturally.
CULTURE: Festivities at {system}. For one day, everyone pretends the sector's problems don't exist. Almost works.
MARKETS: Luxury goods demand surges around {system}. Profiteering complaints filed and immediately shelved.
LABOR: {description}
SUPPLY CHAIN: Production halt at {system} expected to impact downstream systems
LABOR: Workers at {system} demand updated safety equipment. Management offers 'a review committee.' Strike continues.
COMMERCE: Strike at {system} chokes supply lines. Three downstream stations report critical shortages. Nobody's blinking yet.
EDITORIAL: Strike at {system} enters new phase. Corporate press calls it 'disruption.' Workers call it 'survival.'
LABOR: Independent contractors hired to replace striking workers at {system}. Union warns of 'consequences for scabs.'
SCIENCE: {description}
MARKETS: Research breakthrough at {system} sends commodity prices tumbling
SCIENCE: Breakthrough at {system} hailed as 'generational advance.' Patent filings indicate someone plans to profit generationally too.
COMMERCE: New research from {system} renders existing tech obsolete. Investors scramble. Warehouse owners panic.
SCIENCE: Rival labs dispute priority on {system} breakthrough. The discovery belongs to everyone, say the people who didn't fund it.
EDITORIAL: Research breakthrough at {system}. The Collective celebrates. The rest of the sector asks: 'Can we afford it?'
SECURITY: {description}
WARNING: Pirate activity near {system} — independent traders advised to travel armed
SECURITY: Convoy losses near {system} mount. Insurance underwriters refuse new policies for the corridor.
DISTRESS: Two freighters lost near {system} in 48 hours. Debris recovered. Crews not.
SECURITY: Authorities near {system} issue 'travel at own risk' advisory. Patrol vessels reassigned to protect 'priority assets.'
COMMERCE: Shipping rates near {system} surge as carriers demand hazard premiums. Small haulers priced out entirely.
MARKETS: {description}
COMMERCE: {commodity} prices surge at {system} — traders report unusual activity
MARKETS: {commodity} shortage at {system}. Prices climbing. The people who need it most can afford it least.
COMMERCE: Speculation drives {commodity} volatility at {system}. Regulators call for calm. Speculators call their brokers.
MARKETS: {commodity} surplus floods {system}. Sellers undercut each other. Buyers wait for the bottom.
POLITICS: {description}
POLITICS: {description} — analysts predict fallout will reshape trade routes for months.
EDITORIAL: {description} Official statements emphasize stability. Docking bay conversations suggest otherwise.
POLITICS: {description} Independent traders brace for policy shifts. Again.
COMMERCE: {description}
LOCAL: {description} — one more name the sector won't forget.
TRADE: {description} Registry offices take note.
COMMERCE: Independent captain {player} records major sale at {system}. {amount} CR transaction turns heads on the trading floor.
MARKETS: The {ship} docks at {system} with a hold full of {commodity}. Local prices adjust accordingly.
LOCAL: Hauler {player} spotted moving bulk {commodity} through {system}. Competitors take notice.
MINING: Captain {player} reaches depth {detail} in {system} asteroid field. Deep core specialists impressed.
LOCAL: The {ship} returns from {system} belt with a record ore haul. Smelters lining up.
SALVAGE: The {ship} strips a derelict hull clean near {system}. Nothing left but framework and regret.
SECURITY: Captain {player} survives hostile engagement near {system}. Authorities note the wreckage.
INDUSTRY: The forge aboard the {ship} runs hot. Captain {player} reportedly mastering advanced recipes at {system}.
LOCAL: Captain {player} earns top marks at {system}. Station officials call the performance 'exemplary.'
SECURITY: Customs officers at {system} report 'suspicious cargo patterns' from a vessel matching the {ship}'s profile.
Forgeworks foundry output reaches 12-month high

Union leadership lauds declining injury rates — credits improved safety training programs

The Fulcrum denies rumors of expanded military operations — 'routine exercises only'

Rare ore prospecting report: new crystal deposits detected in deep-core surveys
Independent traders association calls for reduced docking fees at Guild-operated stations
Breakstone ore shipments running ahead of schedule — Guild procurement pleased
New restaurant opens on Stellaris Port promenade — reservations already backed up three weeks
Verdant harvest yields break previous records — surplus earmarked for Alliance distribution
DCMC quarterly earnings beat projections — Iron Depths operations cited as key driver
The Aurelia Heritage Foundation acquires new artifacts from pre-Expansion era dig site
Crimson Reach pilots report unusual debris field — origin unknown, salvage opportunities expected
Science Collective publishes annual 'State of Research' report — funding requests up across all departments
Miners' Union apprenticeship program sees highest enrollment in a decade
Alliance storyteller circuit announces new season — performances scheduled across frontier systems
Commerce Guild arbitration court settles long-running tariff dispute — both sides claim victory
Nexus Prime Atmospheric Services reminds residents that recycling surcharges reflect operational costs, not policy decisions
Frontier Alliance reports 14% decrease in missing vessel filings — down to 340 this quarter
DCMC quarterly safety report: zero fatalities in Iron Depths Shafts 1-6. Shafts 7-12 data pending review.
Commerce Guild reminds independent haulers that unlicensed freight carries a minimum 2,000 CR fine per unit
PERSONAL: Seeking crew of the freighter Callaway's Fortune, last seen Crimson Reach corridor. Contact Stellaris Port registry.
BOUNTY POSTED: 4,500 CR for information on cargo theft ring operating between Forgeworks and Breakstone
Oxygen tariffs at Nexus Prime increase for third consecutive quarter — Guild cites 'infrastructure investment'
Iron Depths miners' petition for updated ventilation equipment enters fourth month without response
REDACTED — [Content removed by Commerce Guild Information Bureau pending review] — filed under Administrative Order 7-14
Stellaris Port docking wait times exceed 9 hours — port authority blames 'seasonal volume'
Crimson Reach beacon network offline for scheduled maintenance. Duration: indefinite.
PERSONAL: To the pilot who docked in Bay 14 on Forgeworks last cycle — you left your manifest. And your reputation.
Science Collective denies reports of anomalous readings near Nova Research — 'instrumentation error, nothing more'
Breakstone water reclamation plant operating at 214% rated capacity. Maintenance window: none scheduled.
Three unregistered vessels found derelict along Nexus-Forgeworks corridor. No crew recovered.
Commerce Guild issues statement: 'Price adjustments reflect supply fundamentals, not speculation.' Market analysts disagree.
Haven's Rest food cooperative reports eighth consecutive month of caloric surplus. Neighboring stations less fortunate.
OBITUARY: Captain Ren Vasik, independent hauler, 41. Lost with all hands between Crimson Reach and The Fulcrum.
Miners' Union pension fund audit delayed for second time — Union leadership calls timing 'coincidental'
BOUNTY POSTED: 8,000 CR — armed pirate vessel 'Scorched Policy' — approach with extreme caution
Verdant agricultural output exceeds projections. Commerce Guild expedites exclusive distribution agreement.
Medical supply shipment to Breakstone rerouted through Guild customs. Estimated delay: 11 days.
The Fulcrum garrison strength increased to 'precautionary levels.' No further comment from military command.
PERSONAL: Selling pre-Expansion star charts, provenance unverified. Serious inquiries only. Crimson Reach cantina, ask for Dex.
Axiom Labs requests volunteers for 'routine cognitive study.' Compensation: 200 CR. Side effects described as 'manageable.'
Shipping insurance claims in the Crimson Reach corridor up 40% year-over-year. Underwriters consider exclusion zones.
SIGNAL INTERCEPT [PARTIAL]: ...do not approach... coordinates...compromised... [END FRAGMENT]
Nexus Prime residential sector B-7 reports third consecutive month of air quality violations. Guild cites 'ongoing remediation.'
Independent traders association membership drops 12%. Spokesperson: 'The ones who left aren't trading anymore.'
CORRECTION: Yesterday's report on Forgeworks foundry conditions contained errors. The corrected version has been classified.
